item.title: 中華民國臺灣是什麼概念？中華民國的現況？What is Republic of China (Taiwan)？|#我的學習筆記 #526 ‪‪‪@mynotebooks
item.itunes:title: 中華民國臺灣是什麼概念？中華民國的現況？What is Republic of China (Taiwan)？|#我的學習筆記 #526 ‪‪‪@mynotebooks
item.itunes:author: 我的學習筆記
item.itunes:summary:
Hello! Hello everyone! I am Brother Niu. I don't know if you have ever thought about this. Discuss the current situation of the Republic of China. You may see in the noun. Republic of China in Taiwan. The Republic of China is Taiwan. Or like former President Tsai Ing-wen. Current President Lai Ching-te. The Republic of China Taiwan. But what difference does he make? Why is there such a difference? The difference between the word "在"Is it really big? I know some people might say. This is all word games played by politicians! The discussion is not very meaningful. But in fact. It’s not as simple as you think! Today, let us go through this topic. Let’s talk about the current situation of our Republic of China! Let’s start with the Republic of China in Taiwan. Everyone knows that the Republic of China has a constitution. But unfortunately. Because we lost the begonias, the old hens. Let the current mainland area. Became a fallen area. Therefore, the Constitution of the Republic of China. It is also difficult to implement in the occupied areas. But this does not affect the Republic of China. The existence of the Constitution of the Republic of China. However, the effective jurisdiction of the Republic of China. It is only limited to the Taiwan, Penghu, Kinmen and Matsu areas. But it's just a matter of time. One day the Constitution of the Republic of China. Will return to the mainland. In terms of time. When the Republic of China was officially in Taiwan. Assuming the San Francisco Peace Treaty is in effect. Japan officially abandons Taiwan. Owned from 1952 to today. Almost the Republic of China on the mainland. That is twice the time from 1911 to 1949. More strictly speaking. Basically, if there is no Taiwan. Republic of China. It would not be what it is today. If it weren't for Taiwan. Took in the Republic of China. The Republic of China in 1949. It has disappeared without a trace. There is no way to do it later. Gaining a reputation for democratization. Taiwan. It is a "forced" carrier of the Republic of China system. Let this regime in exile in Taiwan. Survived. But it is also the Republic of China government. The process of continuing to exist in Taiwan. Let Taiwan change the political system of the Republic of China. Or maybe it’s the change of Chinese regime? Let the Republic of China grow. In the past in mainland China. It can be said to be a completely different look. In simple words. Just one grain of rice feeds hundreds of people. In different land and environment. This gave rise to a different system. Social culture. You know, in the 1980s. Non-Party Movements and Social Movements. The most popular time. At that time, many local forces. He even sneered at the Republic of China system. But in the subsequent 1990s. In the process of democratization. Instead, the Republic of China's system in Taiwan. Gained legitimacy.

When our president and legislators. All the people from Taiwan, Penghu, Kinmen and Matsu. Selected in a relatively transparent way. No matter how many flaws there are. At that time, it was also emphasized. The Republic of China is a foreign regime. It will become difficult to stand. After all, this is along the lines of democratization. The process of Taiwanization. At that time, there were many foreign experts and scholars. For our series. The world's first national system change process. Did a lot of research. For example, Japanese scholar Masatake Wakabayashi. This phenomenon is called. Taiwanization of the Republic of China. Some international law scholars also argue that. This democratization process. Taiwan through the Republic of China. Gained national independence. No need to declare independence. The future topics are name rectification and constitution making. Changing the Republic of China from within. To adapt the Republic of China to the needs of Taiwan. Even during the Chen Shui-bian administration. Some scholars also mentioned that the Republic of China in Taiwan. The Second Republic of the Republic of China. Of course there is also the term "Third Republic" But at that time. As Taiwan's local forces gradually grew stronger. Even the calls for Taiwan's independence are getting louder. The provincial groups. Fear of the extinction of the Republic of China. It can only be said that it is increasing. But to be frank. This is processing. Relations between the Republic of China and Taiwan. From the perspective of these so-called "Taiwanese" The Republic of China was. The Kuomintang retreated. Coming to Taiwan. They came to colonize Taiwan. In their eyes, it is an alien regime. But now it has become the Republic of China in Taiwan. Moreover, these local forces. I'm actually running for president. And we must try every possible way to choose. So naturally we have to deal with. Relations between the Republic of China and Taiwan. After all, everyone knows. Early 1970s. America was mired in the Vietnam War. Seeing the Soviet Union's growing power. To deal with the Soviet Union and resolve the Vietnam War. Adopted a policy of reconciliation with the Chinese Communist Party. 1971. Republic of China's seat in the United Nations. Replaced by the Chinese Communist Party. Its international status also declined rapidly. The Chinese Communist Party is internationally regarded as. The only legitimate government in China. The Republic of China in the International. Lost the basis for representing China. The Kuomintang's policy of unification of China. Instead, it became a restriction. The stumbling block to the development of our Republic of China. The CCP even uses the "One China" principle. Constantly belittling the international status of the Republic of China. Threatening our survival. The International Status of the Republic of China. In fact, it is just like cross-strait relations. It's our ruling authorities. Problems that must be faced directly. Of course after. When dealing with these issues. Because of the complicated relationship. with the Chinese Communist Party.

Let the relationship between the Republic of China and Taiwan. Hidden again. How to locate the Republic of China. Or the international status of "Taiwan"? This has always been a hot potato. After Chen Shui-bian came to power. Emphasis on the two-nation theory. As a result, cross-strait relations have reached a stalemate. Until the Ma Ying-jeou government came to power. One China advocacy for cross-strait relations. Treat Taiwan as a region, not a country. So at that time. The Kuomintang's "Republic of China Taiwan" The DPP’s “The Republic of China is Taiwan”. Even more so, "Republic of China Taiwan" These two sets of concepts confront each other. Let's discuss this first. Taiwan, Republic of China. The Kuomintang's claim is. According to the Constitution. Both sides of the Taiwan Strait belong to one China. Even in 1997. The constitution has been amended. Divided the Republic of China into two parts. Taiwan area is Taiwan, Penghu, Kinmen and Matsu. The mainland is. Territory other than Taiwan, Penghu, Kinmen and Matsu. Whether in Taiwan or mainland China. Under the Constitution, they all belong to one China. The Constitution of the Republic of China. It's actually written like this. Even though the Republic of China. and the People's Republic of China. Currently not affiliated. It is an objective fact. The current situation in the Taiwan Strait. It is also internationally recognized. But Brother Niu based on the Constitution. I still have to say one thing. "Communism and independence" are doomed to fail! Unification is unstoppable! In this case. Then why not amend the Constitution to suit the current situation? So the Kuomintang. I also cling to the Constitution. Since you feel that it does not conform to the current situation. Then let’s amend the constitution! But everyone knows. In today's international situation. And under the situation in the Taiwan Strait. Constitutional amendments related to cross-strait relations. It will only be the last straw that triggers a war. Any party that proposes constitutional amendment at this stage. It would just be simply irresponsible. So in 1992. Lee Teng-hui's Administration. Republic of China and People's Republic of China. Straits Exchange Foundation. Association for Relations Across the Taiwan Straits. That is what we call the Straits Exchange Foundation. and the Association for Relations Across the Taiwan Straits. Two sessions hold talks in Hong Kong. The result of this meeting was later called the 1992 Consensus. But everyone! Did you know that this meeting actually produced no results? This meeting was supposed to discuss. Some cooperation matters in the correspondence. between the two parties. During the negotiation process, Beijing demanded. Taiwan must accept. The One China Principle: Both Sides. of the Taiwan Straits belong to One China. The Chinese authorities believe. One China is the People's Republic of China. After the unification in the future. Taiwan will become a "special administrative region" under its jurisdiction.

We believe that "One China" Should refer to the Republic of China since its establishment in 1912. Its sovereignty extends to the whole of China. But the current governance. It only applies to Taiwan, Penghu, Kinmen and Matsu. Taiwan is part of China. But the mainland is also part of China. We are stating. One China principle signed. Write down the Republic of China calendar. This was unacceptable to the Beijing authorities. So back and forth many times. There is no result after all. As for how it appeared later. What about the term "1992 Consensus"? The term "1992 Consensus" originated from the 1999. Tang Shubei, then vice president of the China Association. for Relations Across the Taiwan Straits, claimed. The two sides of the Taiwan Strait had already discussed in 1992. Reaching a "One China Consensus" This is the first time that the above meeting content. The beginning of what is generally called “consensus”. Later, he served as Chairman of the Mainland Affairs Council. of the Executive Yuan. Su Qi on April 28, 2000. For the first time, the above meeting content. Generally referred to as the "1992 Consensus" And add the interpretation of "One China, Different Interpretations" But from the above statement. We can easily see. For the Chinese Communist Party. 1992. Main Aspects of Negotiation Results. It's about "One China" But the government of the Republic of China at that time. The emphasis is on "each expressing himself" But the CCP did not agree. The Chinese Communist Party authorities. summarized the 1992 Consensus as. Both sides of the Taiwan Strait adhere to the one-China principle. Working together for national reunification. Both sides jointly oppose. Two Chinas, One China, One Taiwan. Believe that there is only one China in the world. Government of the People's Republic of China. It is the only legitimate government representing all of China. Government of the People's Republic of China. Represents all of China. The only legitimate government. Taiwan is Chinese territory. An integral part. Therefore, in the CCP’s view. The 1992 Consensus cannot be interpreted as. One China, Different Interpretations. Nor can it be interpreted as. Can recognize the sovereignty and existence of the Republic of China. It is even more impossible to accept. One China is the Republic of China. The Republic of China is also China. Taiwan belongs to the Republic of China. Taiwan is a sovereign and independent country. Taiwan does not belong to the People's Republic of China, etc. These political statements and actions. Oppose Two Chinas, One China, One Taiwan. Chinese independence, Taiwanese independence, etc. The 1992 Consensus only has "One China" There is no "each table" However, under this premise. Former KMT Chairman Lien Chan. Also in 2005. Visited mainland China. With the General Secretary of the CPC Central Committee. After the meeting with Hu Jintao. The "1992 Consensus" was once again proposed. As for what they said. Is it the same 1992 consensus? No one knows.

Do you want to listen to what you are saying? I know that. Everyone is still confused. Brother Niu, let me give you an example. Talk about the past period. The Dragon Boat Festival rice dumplings are ready. The Kuomintang advocates. My zongzi is northern zongzi. The Chinese Communist Party advocates. His zongzi is southern zongzi. But the Kuomintang feels. Southern Zongzi is basically steamed rice cake. I don't want to admit that he is a zongzi. The Chinese Communist Party also advocates. Northern rice dumplings are basically 3D oily rice! But in order to have a good Dragon Boat Festival. We can still agree reluctantly. The other party is a zongzi! most of the time. It's an art not to reveal it. The 1992 Consensus has always been regarded as. The political foundation for the development of cross-strait relations. Under the unanimous recognition. of their respective "1992 Consensus" The two sides of the Taiwan Strait have achieved a closer economy. As well as cultural exchange and cooperation. Year 2008. Direct sea and air transport and direct mail service between the two sides. of the Taiwan Strait. Realizing the “Three Links”. year 2010. The two sides signed ECFA. Although I have stopped a lot of lists recently. They are holding the banner of the 1992 Consensus. In Singapore in 2015. The first Ma-Xi meeting was held. But the connotation of the "1992 Consensus" It seems to be changing. Early 2019. Xi Jinping, the leader of the Chinese Communist Party. On the 40th Anniversary of the "Message to Compatriots in Taiwan" Delivered a speech at the memorial meeting. He advocated cross-strait policy. Must be based on the "1992 Consensus" Reiterate the need to abide by the "one country, two systems" principle. Once Xi Jinping puts the 1992 Consensus. Interpretation of "One Country, Two Systems" The one country is of course the CCP. There is no room for individual expression. This also awakened the people. How come this seems to be the past? The 1992 Consensus advocated by the Kuomintang. is not quite the same. Discover the greatest consensus of the 1992 Consensus. There is no consensus. When it comes to "one country, two systems" The best example is Hong Kong. But Hong Kong has been. After the Basic Law was amended. It has also become a one country, one system. From Taiwan's public opinion to geopolitics. And recently, American arms dealers came to Taiwan. Looking for partners. There are also AI technology industries preparing to enter Taiwan. In these aspects. At present, the "1992 Consensus" Relatively large space. Maybe it's getting less and less. After talking about the Kuomintang’s point of view. Next, let’s talk about the Democratic Progressive Party! Democratic Progressive Party vs. Kuomintang. The "1992 Consensus" Another argument. Taiwan's future is determined by the Taiwanese people. The main point of this discussion is. Taiwan, Republic of China. A sovereign and independent country.

Not affiliated with the People's Republic of China. Sovereignty belongs to the 23 million people of Taiwan. Only the Taiwanese people. Have the right to decide Taiwan's future. The Republic of China is Taiwan. The Democratic Progressive Party believes. Taiwan can only throw off the shackles of China. To move towards a normal country. Whether you agree or not. Or the means to achieve this end. There are concerns that it may lead to war. But he said plain words. Your future is determined by you. they think. As the Chinese Communist Party authorities press forward. Forced the party to start. The Republic of China can't care. Also consider the voice of the Republic of Taiwan. Since it is not feasible to mention the Republic of China. It is even more impossible to mention the Republic of Taiwan. Taiwan's statement. It became a political ideal. The result of compromise with political reality. Let the Republic of China and Taiwan. The concept has become the same group. The new generation of Taiwanese young people identify with Taiwan. But it does not exclude the Republic of China. Related symbols. These symbols are gradually lost. History and territory with mainland China. Sovereign Link. The trend of "de-Sinicization" The concept of the Republic of China (Taiwan). The Republic of China is equal to Taiwan. Completely decoupled from One China and Chinese representation. Meaning Taiwan is not part of China. The concept of the Republic of China (Taiwan). It has gone beyond the framework of "One China" What the CCP thinks. Taiwan is part of China. The CCP believes that Taiwan. A province of the People's Republic of China. The Kuomintang believes. Taiwan is part of China. It is also part of the Republic of China. But the Democratic Progressive Party believes. that "Republic of China Taiwan" A sovereign and independent country. This concept combines the Republic of China and Taiwan. The three meanings of being a sovereign and independent country. In the Democratic Progressive Party’s view. Republic of China and Taiwan Independence. No longer mutually exclusive concepts. But we can tolerate each other. They cleverly turned the independence. of the Republic of China into. And Taiwan's independence. Concepts such as "One Country, Two Systems" and "1992 Consensus" Convert and label. Trying to make the Republic of China. Equivalent to Taiwan independence. One Country, Two Systems is equivalent to the 1992 Consensus. But legally speaking. The Republic of China does not mean that Taiwan is independent. One Country, Two Systems and the 1992 Consensus. It involves cross-strait relations and institutional arrangements. The two have very different meanings. The Democratic Progressive Party believes. They have pragmatically put the Republic of China, Taiwan. Becoming the mainstream discourse within the party. and in Taiwanese society. But the CCP still criticizes this as gradual Taiwan independence. It is believed that this will increase hostility between the two sides. of the Taiwan Strait. Firmly oppose any form of Taiwan independence. The Democratic Progressive Party may think.

Since it cannot be used internationally. The name of the Republic of China. The Chinese Communist Party also does not recognize the existence. of the Republic of China. For the safety of Taiwan. Avoid clear and present danger. The DPP was also unable to complete the referendum. The goal of establishing the Republic of Taiwan. Therefore, the Republic of China, Taiwan. Instead, it has become the biggest consensus in Taiwanese society. After discussing both sides. Finally, let's talk about. The International Status of the Republic of China. Taiwan transformed into the Republic of China. Following the Republic of China in mainland China. How different. and the discontinuity between them. this is very important. After all, the Chinese Communist Party claims that the Republic of China. It was destroyed as early as 1949. And he was eager to help the Republic of China compile history books. Everyone should know. Every time China changes dynasties. First things first. That is to compile history books. Then the Republic of China Taiwan. Disconnection from the Republic of China. In fact, it does not violate the CCP’s theory. On the one hand, the Chinese Communist Party claims. The Republic of China has been destroyed. But they also demanded that Taiwan abide by the Constitution. of the Republic of China. No name change, no Taiwan independence. This self-contradictory logic. They want to use the Republic of China to hold Taiwan. So in 2022. Speaker of the United States House of Representatives. Pelosi came to Taiwan and made a call. After the passage of the Taiwan Policy Act. Why is the CCP so angry? Jump up and launch the military exercise around Taiwan. Because of the U.S. Taiwan Policy Act. It will bring us closer to legal independence. Because now we are de facto independent. Neither party has advocated "legal independence" After the United States passed this law. There might actually be some changes. And it also makes the relationship between Taiwan and the United States. Transformed into a relationship between countries. This is what the CCP doesn't want to see. Clarifying the relationship between the Republic of China and Taiwan. It is also for our international space. Due to the one-China policy in the international. The Republic of China government is not recognized. We can represent China. If we want to use the Republic of China (Taiwan). Traveling internationally. It is necessary to point out the difference with the Republic of China. And I no longer want to represent China. It only represents the position of the Republic of China or Taiwan. Republic of China system. If you want to be Taiwanese. Constitutional reform is an inevitable path. Taiwan will need a. A constitution more in line with current conditions. In particular, the Taiwanization of the Republic of China system. The path of constitutional amendment and constitution making. It's almost unavoidable. I wonder what you think? alright! That's it for this episode. If you like. Please help us press the subscribe button and share it. If you have capable friends. You can also join our membership. Or click Super Thanks! In addition, our DC group is now open!

Welcome everyone to come in and chat. My study notes. See you next time. ByeBye！

====

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mmiypn7urww

討論到現在中華民國的處境

在名詞上可能會看到

中華民國在臺灣、中華民國是臺灣

又或是之前像是前總統蔡英文

現任總統賴清德提的「中華民國臺灣」

但他有什麼差別呢？

又為什麼會有這樣的差別？

而有這個「在」字的差別

到底大不大呢？

我知道有人可能會說

這都是政客他們的文字遊戲啦

討論的意義並不大

但其實實際上

並沒有想像的那麼簡單哦

今天就讓我們透過這個議題

來講講我們中華民國的現況吧！

#臺灣 #台灣 #中華民國 #中國 #中華民國臺灣 #Taiwan #公民 #社會 #歷史 #政治 #賴清德 #🇹🇼 #民進黨 #總統 #憲法 #憲政 #台獨 #臺獨 #台獨黨綱 #修憲 #中共 #互不隸屬 #國民黨 #民眾黨 #九二共識 #蔡英文 #馬英九 #李登輝

📍讓我們繼續活下去 ► http://bit.ly/2tGL3eB

LINE貼圖搜尋「我的學習筆記」或是點下方連結

我的學習筆記2

https://reurl.cc/qkk38y

我的學習筆記3

https://reurl.cc/n77Rn6

我的學習筆記4

https://reurl.cc/mllNq9

🎬更多的學習筆記

YOUTUBE長影片討論 ► https://reurl.cc/gy2ZR

Tiktok上更多短影片 ► https://reurl.cc/bkjjz3

Facebook關注更多消息 ► https://bit.ly/2TFDZXC

Instagram掌握國際大事 ► https://reurl.cc/GyxEp

方格子學術公眾化成果 ► http://bit.ly/32J6mrK

DisCord群組一起聊天抬槓►https://discord.gg/YmSkbSVrUC

小額支付贊助我歐付寶►http://bit.ly/2Z1s1yA

當然還有PayPal►PayPal.Me/wMyNotebook

😉特別感謝5月份支持學習筆記的朋友

Polya Yang、柯誠譽、Rosemary 、阿里不打、Sion Ein、Roots Ko、Wu Pin-Ying、赤炎、懶虎提姆閣、Roy Li、KaHu Lau、葉小雲、李文傑、陳建宏、Louis Tseng、薩麥爾_MLBB、祈翠、Mariposa Bay、語禪、Krib Stek、張輔庭、whiskyhot886 、Chen Chong Lee、Charlie曾、蔡岳峰、Chung-Ming Wang、上善若水、林雋凱

📍特別感謝每位默默支持我們的朋友們，你們給的不只是錢財，更是一段友誼 當你像小蝦米一樣對抗大鯨魚，當你的敵人遠比你想像中強大的時候 發現了這個世界，其實有一群未曾相識卻力挺你的朋友，我想，這就是世界上最美好的感覺了
item.description:
Hello! Hello everyone! I am Brother Niu. I don't know if you have ever thought about this. Discuss the current situation of the Republic of China. You may see in the noun. Republic of China in Taiwan. The Republic of China is Taiwan. Or like former President Tsai Ing-wen. Current President Lai Ching-te. The Republic of China Taiwan. But what difference does he make? Why is there such a difference? The difference between the word "在"Is it really big? I know some people might say. This is all word games played by politicians! The discussion is not very meaningful. But in fact. It’s not as simple as you think! Today, let us go through this topic. Let’s talk about the current situation of our Republic of China! Let’s start with the Republic of China in Taiwan. Everyone knows that the Republic of China has a constitution. But unfortunately. Because we lost the begonias, the old hens. Let the current mainland area. Became a fallen area. Therefore, the Constitution of the Republic of China. It is also difficult to implement in the occupied areas. But this does not affect the Republic of China. The existence of the Constitution of the Republic of China. However, the effective jurisdiction of the Republic of China. It is only limited to the Taiwan, Penghu, Kinmen and Matsu areas. But it's just a matter of time. One day the Constitution of the Republic of China. Will return to the mainland. In terms of time. When the Republic of China was officially in Taiwan. Assuming the San Francisco Peace Treaty is in effect. Japan officially abandons Taiwan. Owned from 1952 to today. Almost the Republic of China on the mainland. That is twice the time from 1911 to 1949. More strictly speaking. Basically, if there is no Taiwan. Republic of China. It would not be what it is today. If it weren't for Taiwan. Took in the Republic of China. The Republic of China in 1949. It has disappeared without a trace. There is no way to do it later. Gaining a reputation for democratization. Taiwan. It is a "forced" carrier of the Republic of China system. Let this regime in exile in Taiwan. Survived. But it is also the Republic of China government. The process of continuing to exist in Taiwan. Let Taiwan change the political system of the Republic of China. Or maybe it’s the change of Chinese regime? Let the Republic of China grow. In the past in mainland China. It can be said to be a completely different look. In simple words. Just one grain of rice feeds hundreds of people. In different land and environment. This gave rise to a different system. Social culture. You know, in the 1980s. Non-Party Movements and Social Movements. The most popular time. At that time, many local forces. He even sneered at the Republic of China system. But in the subsequent 1990s. In the process of democratization. Instead, the Republic of China's system in Taiwan. Gained legitimacy.

When our president and legislators. All the people from Taiwan, Penghu, Kinmen and Matsu. Selected in a relatively transparent way. No matter how many flaws there are. At that time, it was also emphasized. The Republic of China is a foreign regime. It will become difficult to stand. After all, this is along the lines of democratization. The process of Taiwanization. At that time, there were many foreign experts and scholars. For our series. The world's first national system change process. Did a lot of research. For example, Japanese scholar Masatake Wakabayashi. This phenomenon is called. Taiwanization of the Republic of China. Some international law scholars also argue that. This democratization process. Taiwan through the Republic of China. Gained national independence. No need to declare independence. The future topics are name rectification and constitution making. Changing the Republic of China from within. To adapt the Republic of China to the needs of Taiwan. Even during the Chen Shui-bian administration. Some scholars also mentioned that the Republic of China in Taiwan. The Second Republic of the Republic of China. Of course there is also the term "Third Republic" But at that time. As Taiwan's local forces gradually grew stronger. Even the calls for Taiwan's independence are getting louder. The provincial groups. Fear of the extinction of the Republic of China. It can only be said that it is increasing. But to be frank. This is processing. Relations between the Republic of China and Taiwan. From the perspective of these so-called "Taiwanese" The Republic of China was. The Kuomintang retreated. Coming to Taiwan. They came to colonize Taiwan. In their eyes, it is an alien regime. But now it has become the Republic of China in Taiwan. Moreover, these local forces. I'm actually running for president. And we must try every possible way to choose. So naturally we have to deal with. Relations between the Republic of China and Taiwan. After all, everyone knows. Early 1970s. America was mired in the Vietnam War. Seeing the Soviet Union's growing power. To deal with the Soviet Union and resolve the Vietnam War. Adopted a policy of reconciliation with the Chinese Communist Party. 1971. Republic of China's seat in the United Nations. Replaced by the Chinese Communist Party. Its international status also declined rapidly. The Chinese Communist Party is internationally regarded as. The only legitimate government in China. The Republic of China in the International. Lost the basis for representing China. The Kuomintang's policy of unification of China. Instead, it became a restriction. The stumbling block to the development of our Republic of China. The CCP even uses the "One China" principle. Constantly belittling the international status of the Republic of China. Threatening our survival. The International Status of the Republic of China. In fact, it is just like cross-strait relations. It's our ruling authorities. Problems that must be faced directly. Of course after. When dealing with these issues. Because of the complicated relationship. with the Chinese Communist Party.

Let the relationship between the Republic of China and Taiwan. Hidden again. How to locate the Republic of China. Or the international status of "Taiwan"? This has always been a hot potato. After Chen Shui-bian came to power. Emphasis on the two-nation theory. As a result, cross-strait relations have reached a stalemate. Until the Ma Ying-jeou government came to power. One China advocacy for cross-strait relations. Treat Taiwan as a region, not a country. So at that time. The Kuomintang's "Republic of China Taiwan" The DPP’s “The Republic of China is Taiwan”. Even more so, "Republic of China Taiwan" These two sets of concepts confront each other. Let's discuss this first. Taiwan, Republic of China. The Kuomintang's claim is. According to the Constitution. Both sides of the Taiwan Strait belong to one China. Even in 1997. The constitution has been amended. Divided the Republic of China into two parts. Taiwan area is Taiwan, Penghu, Kinmen and Matsu. The mainland is. Territory other than Taiwan, Penghu, Kinmen and Matsu. Whether in Taiwan or mainland China. Under the Constitution, they all belong to one China. The Constitution of the Republic of China. It's actually written like this. Even though the Republic of China. and the People's Republic of China. Currently not affiliated. It is an objective fact. The current situation in the Taiwan Strait. It is also internationally recognized. But Brother Niu based on the Constitution. I still have to say one thing. "Communism and independence" are doomed to fail! Unification is unstoppable! In this case. Then why not amend the Constitution to suit the current situation? So the Kuomintang. I also cling to the Constitution. Since you feel that it does not conform to the current situation. Then let’s amend the constitution! But everyone knows. In today's international situation. And under the situation in the Taiwan Strait. Constitutional amendments related to cross-strait relations. It will only be the last straw that triggers a war. Any party that proposes constitutional amendment at this stage. It would just be simply irresponsible. So in 1992. Lee Teng-hui's Administration. Republic of China and People's Republic of China. Straits Exchange Foundation. Association for Relations Across the Taiwan Straits. That is what we call the Straits Exchange Foundation. and the Association for Relations Across the Taiwan Straits. Two sessions hold talks in Hong Kong. The result of this meeting was later called the 1992 Consensus. But everyone! Did you know that this meeting actually produced no results? This meeting was supposed to discuss. Some cooperation matters in the correspondence. between the two parties. During the negotiation process, Beijing demanded. Taiwan must accept. The One China Principle: Both Sides. of the Taiwan Straits belong to One China. The Chinese authorities believe. One China is the People's Republic of China. After the unification in the future. Taiwan will become a "special administrative region" under its jurisdiction.

We believe that "One China" Should refer to the Republic of China since its establishment in 1912. Its sovereignty extends to the whole of China. But the current governance. It only applies to Taiwan, Penghu, Kinmen and Matsu. Taiwan is part of China. But the mainland is also part of China. We are stating. One China principle signed. Write down the Republic of China calendar. This was unacceptable to the Beijing authorities. So back and forth many times. There is no result after all. As for how it appeared later. What about the term "1992 Consensus"? The term "1992 Consensus" originated from the 1999. Tang Shubei, then vice president of the China Association. for Relations Across the Taiwan Straits, claimed. The two sides of the Taiwan Strait had already discussed in 1992. Reaching a "One China Consensus" This is the first time that the above meeting content. The beginning of what is generally called “consensus”. Later, he served as Chairman of the Mainland Affairs Council. of the Executive Yuan. Su Qi on April 28, 2000. For the first time, the above meeting content. Generally referred to as the "1992 Consensus" And add the interpretation of "One China, Different Interpretations" But from the above statement. We can easily see. For the Chinese Communist Party. 1992. Main Aspects of Negotiation Results. It's about "One China" But the government of the Republic of China at that time. The emphasis is on "each expressing himself" But the CCP did not agree. The Chinese Communist Party authorities. summarized the 1992 Consensus as. Both sides of the Taiwan Strait adhere to the one-China principle. Working together for national reunification. Both sides jointly oppose. Two Chinas, One China, One Taiwan. Believe that there is only one China in the world. Government of the People's Republic of China. It is the only legitimate government representing all of China. Government of the People's Republic of China. Represents all of China. The only legitimate government. Taiwan is Chinese territory. An integral part. Therefore, in the CCP’s view. The 1992 Consensus cannot be interpreted as. One China, Different Interpretations. Nor can it be interpreted as. Can recognize the sovereignty and existence of the Republic of China. It is even more impossible to accept. One China is the Republic of China. The Republic of China is also China. Taiwan belongs to the Republic of China. Taiwan is a sovereign and independent country. Taiwan does not belong to the People's Republic of China, etc. These political statements and actions. Oppose Two Chinas, One China, One Taiwan. Chinese independence, Taiwanese independence, etc. The 1992 Consensus only has "One China" There is no "each table" However, under this premise. Former KMT Chairman Lien Chan. Also in 2005. Visited mainland China. With the General Secretary of the CPC Central Committee. After the meeting with Hu Jintao. The "1992 Consensus" was once again proposed. As for what they said. Is it the same 1992 consensus? No one knows.

Do you want to listen to what you are saying? I know that. Everyone is still confused. Brother Niu, let me give you an example. Talk about the past period. The Dragon Boat Festival rice dumplings are ready. The Kuomintang advocates. My zongzi is northern zongzi. The Chinese Communist Party advocates. His zongzi is southern zongzi. But the Kuomintang feels. Southern Zongzi is basically steamed rice cake. I don't want to admit that he is a zongzi. The Chinese Communist Party also advocates. Northern rice dumplings are basically 3D oily rice! But in order to have a good Dragon Boat Festival. We can still agree reluctantly. The other party is a zongzi! most of the time. It's an art not to reveal it. The 1992 Consensus has always been regarded as. The political foundation for the development of cross-strait relations. Under the unanimous recognition. of their respective "1992 Consensus" The two sides of the Taiwan Strait have achieved a closer economy. As well as cultural exchange and cooperation. Year 2008. Direct sea and air transport and direct mail service between the two sides. of the Taiwan Strait. Realizing the “Three Links”. year 2010. The two sides signed ECFA. Although I have stopped a lot of lists recently. They are holding the banner of the 1992 Consensus. In Singapore in 2015. The first Ma-Xi meeting was held. But the connotation of the "1992 Consensus" It seems to be changing. Early 2019. Xi Jinping, the leader of the Chinese Communist Party. On the 40th Anniversary of the "Message to Compatriots in Taiwan" Delivered a speech at the memorial meeting. He advocated cross-strait policy. Must be based on the "1992 Consensus" Reiterate the need to abide by the "one country, two systems" principle. Once Xi Jinping puts the 1992 Consensus. Interpretation of "One Country, Two Systems" The one country is of course the CCP. There is no room for individual expression. This also awakened the people. How come this seems to be the past? The 1992 Consensus advocated by the Kuomintang. is not quite the same. Discover the greatest consensus of the 1992 Consensus. There is no consensus. When it comes to "one country, two systems" The best example is Hong Kong. But Hong Kong has been. After the Basic Law was amended. It has also become a one country, one system. From Taiwan's public opinion to geopolitics. And recently, American arms dealers came to Taiwan. Looking for partners. There are also AI technology industries preparing to enter Taiwan. In these aspects. At present, the "1992 Consensus" Relatively large space. Maybe it's getting less and less. After talking about the Kuomintang’s point of view. Next, let’s talk about the Democratic Progressive Party! Democratic Progressive Party vs. Kuomintang. The "1992 Consensus" Another argument. Taiwan's future is determined by the Taiwanese people. The main point of this discussion is. Taiwan, Republic of China. A sovereign and independent country.

Not affiliated with the People's Republic of China. Sovereignty belongs to the 23 million people of Taiwan. Only the Taiwanese people. Have the right to decide Taiwan's future. The Republic of China is Taiwan. The Democratic Progressive Party believes. Taiwan can only throw off the shackles of China. To move towards a normal country. Whether you agree or not. Or the means to achieve this end. There are concerns that it may lead to war. But he said plain words. Your future is determined by you. they think. As the Chinese Communist Party authorities press forward. Forced the party to start. The Republic of China can't care. Also consider the voice of the Republic of Taiwan. Since it is not feasible to mention the Republic of China. It is even more impossible to mention the Republic of Taiwan. Taiwan's statement. It became a political ideal. The result of compromise with political reality. Let the Republic of China and Taiwan. The concept has become the same group. The new generation of Taiwanese young people identify with Taiwan. But it does not exclude the Republic of China. Related symbols. These symbols are gradually lost. History and territory with mainland China. Sovereign Link. The trend of "de-Sinicization" The concept of the Republic of China (Taiwan). The Republic of China is equal to Taiwan. Completely decoupled from One China and Chinese representation. Meaning Taiwan is not part of China. The concept of the Republic of China (Taiwan). It has gone beyond the framework of "One China" What the CCP thinks. Taiwan is part of China. The CCP believes that Taiwan. A province of the People's Republic of China. The Kuomintang believes. Taiwan is part of China. It is also part of the Republic of China. But the Democratic Progressive Party believes. that "Republic of China Taiwan" A sovereign and independent country. This concept combines the Republic of China and Taiwan. The three meanings of being a sovereign and independent country. In the Democratic Progressive Party’s view. Republic of China and Taiwan Independence. No longer mutually exclusive concepts. But we can tolerate each other. They cleverly turned the independence. of the Republic of China into. And Taiwan's independence. Concepts such as "One Country, Two Systems" and "1992 Consensus" Convert and label. Trying to make the Republic of China. Equivalent to Taiwan independence. One Country, Two Systems is equivalent to the 1992 Consensus. But legally speaking. The Republic of China does not mean that Taiwan is independent. One Country, Two Systems and the 1992 Consensus. It involves cross-strait relations and institutional arrangements. The two have very different meanings. The Democratic Progressive Party believes. They have pragmatically put the Republic of China, Taiwan. Becoming the mainstream discourse within the party. and in Taiwanese society. But the CCP still criticizes this as gradual Taiwan independence. It is believed that this will increase hostility between the two sides. of the Taiwan Strait. Firmly oppose any form of Taiwan independence. The Democratic Progressive Party may think.

Since it cannot be used internationally. The name of the Republic of China. The Chinese Communist Party also does not recognize the existence. of the Republic of China. For the safety of Taiwan. Avoid clear and present danger. The DPP was also unable to complete the referendum. The goal of establishing the Republic of Taiwan. Therefore, the Republic of China, Taiwan. Instead, it has become the biggest consensus in Taiwanese society. After discussing both sides. Finally, let's talk about. The International Status of the Republic of China. Taiwan transformed into the Republic of China. Following the Republic of China in mainland China. How different. and the discontinuity between them. this is very important. After all, the Chinese Communist Party claims that the Republic of China. It was destroyed as early as 1949. And he was eager to help the Republic of China compile history books. Everyone should know. Every time China changes dynasties. First things first. That is to compile history books. Then the Republic of China Taiwan. Disconnection from the Republic of China. In fact, it does not violate the CCP’s theory. On the one hand, the Chinese Communist Party claims. The Republic of China has been destroyed. But they also demanded that Taiwan abide by the Constitution. of the Republic of China. No name change, no Taiwan independence. This self-contradictory logic. They want to use the Republic of China to hold Taiwan. So in 2022. Speaker of the United States House of Representatives. Pelosi came to Taiwan and made a call. After the passage of the Taiwan Policy Act. Why is the CCP so angry? Jump up and launch the military exercise around Taiwan. Because of the U.S. Taiwan Policy Act. It will bring us closer to legal independence. Because now we are de facto independent. Neither party has advocated "legal independence" After the United States passed this law. There might actually be some changes. And it also makes the relationship between Taiwan and the United States. Transformed into a relationship between countries. This is what the CCP doesn't want to see. Clarifying the relationship between the Republic of China and Taiwan. It is also for our international space. Due to the one-China policy in the international. The Republic of China government is not recognized. We can represent China. If we want to use the Republic of China (Taiwan). Traveling internationally. It is necessary to point out the difference with the Republic of China. And I no longer want to represent China. It only represents the position of the Republic of China or Taiwan. Republic of China system. If you want to be Taiwanese. Constitutional reform is an inevitable path. Taiwan will need a. A constitution more in line with current conditions. In particular, the Taiwanization of the Republic of China system. The path of constitutional amendment and constitution making. It's almost unavoidable. I wonder what you think? alright! That's it for this episode. If you like. Please help us press the subscribe button and share it. If you have capable friends. You can also join our membership. Or click Super Thanks! In addition, our DC group is now open!

Welcome everyone to come in and chat. My study notes. See you next time. ByeBye！

====

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mmiypn7urww

討論到現在中華民國的處境

在名詞上可能會看到

中華民國在臺灣、中華民國是臺灣

又或是之前像是前總統蔡英文

現任總統賴清德提的「中華民國臺灣」

但他有什麼差別呢？

又為什麼會有這樣的差別？

而有這個「在」字的差別

到底大不大呢？

我知道有人可能會說

這都是政客他們的文字遊戲啦

討論的意義並不大

但其實實際上

並沒有想像的那麼簡單哦

今天就讓我們透過這個議題

來講講我們中華民國的現況吧！

#臺灣 #台灣 #中華民國 #中國 #中華民國臺灣 #Taiwan #公民 #社會 #歷史 #政治 #賴清德 #🇹🇼 #民進黨 #總統 #憲法 #憲政 #台獨 #臺獨 #台獨黨綱 #修憲 #中共 #互不隸屬 #國民黨 #民眾黨 #九二共識 #蔡英文 #馬英九 #李登輝

📍讓我們繼續活下去 ► http://bit.ly/2tGL3eB

LINE貼圖搜尋「我的學習筆記」或是點下方連結

我的學習筆記2

https://reurl.cc/qkk38y

我的學習筆記3

https://reurl.cc/n77Rn6

我的學習筆記4

https://reurl.cc/mllNq9

🎬更多的學習筆記

YOUTUBE長影片討論 ► https://reurl.cc/gy2ZR

Tiktok上更多短影片 ► https://reurl.cc/bkjjz3

Facebook關注更多消息 ► https://bit.ly/2TFDZXC

Instagram掌握國際大事 ► https://reurl.cc/GyxEp

方格子學術公眾化成果 ► http://bit.ly/32J6mrK

DisCord群組一起聊天抬槓►https://discord.gg/YmSkbSVrUC

小額支付贊助我歐付寶►http://bit.ly/2Z1s1yA

當然還有PayPal►PayPal.Me/wMyNotebook

😉特別感謝5月份支持學習筆記的朋友

Polya Yang、柯誠譽、Rosemary 、阿里不打、Sion Ein、Roots Ko、Wu Pin-Ying、赤炎、懶虎提姆閣、Roy Li、KaHu Lau、葉小雲、李文傑、陳建宏、Louis Tseng、薩麥爾_MLBB、祈翠、Mariposa Bay、語禪、Krib Stek、張輔庭、whiskyhot886 、Chen Chong Lee、Charlie曾、蔡岳峰、Chung-Ming Wang、上善若水、林雋凱

📍特別感謝每位默默支持我們的朋友們，你們給的不只是錢財，更是一段友誼 當你像小蝦米一樣對抗大鯨魚，當你的敵人遠比你想像中強大的時候 發現了這個世界，其實有一群未曾相識卻力挺你的朋友，我想，這就是世界上最美好的感覺了
item.content:encoded:
Hello! Hello everyone! I am Brother Niu. I don't know if you have ever thought about this. Discuss the current situation of the Republic of China. You may see in the noun. Republic of China in Taiwan. The Republic of China is Taiwan. Or like former President Tsai Ing-wen. Current President Lai Ching-te. The Republic of China Taiwan. But what difference does he make? Why is there such a difference? The difference between the word "在"Is it really big? I know some people might say. This is all word games played by politicians! The discussion is not very meaningful. But in fact. It’s not as simple as you think! Today, let us go through this topic. Let’s talk about the current situation of our Republic of China! Let’s start with the Republic of China in Taiwan. Everyone knows that the Republic of China has a constitution. But unfortunately. Because we lost the begonias, the old hens. Let the current mainland area. Became a fallen area. Therefore, the Constitution of the Republic of China. It is also difficult to implement in the occupied areas. But this does not affect the Republic of China. The existence of the Constitution of the Republic of China. However, the effective jurisdiction of the Republic of China. It is only limited to the Taiwan, Penghu, Kinmen and Matsu areas. But it's just a matter of time. One day the Constitution of the Republic of China. Will return to the mainland. In terms of time. When the Republic of China was officially in Taiwan. Assuming the San Francisco Peace Treaty is in effect. Japan officially abandons Taiwan. Owned from 1952 to today. Almost the Republic of China on the mainland. That is twice the time from 1911 to 1949. More strictly speaking. Basically, if there is no Taiwan. Republic of China. It would not be what it is today. If it weren't for Taiwan. Took in the Republic of China. The Republic of China in 1949. It has disappeared without a trace. There is no way to do it later. Gaining a reputation for democratization. Taiwan. It is a "forced" carrier of the Republic of China system. Let this regime in exile in Taiwan. Survived. But it is also the Republic of China government. The process of continuing to exist in Taiwan. Let Taiwan change the political system of the Republic of China. Or maybe it’s the change of Chinese regime? Let the Republic of China grow. In the past in mainland China. It can be said to be a completely different look. In simple words. Just one grain of rice feeds hundreds of people. In different land and environment. This gave rise to a different system. Social culture. You know, in the 1980s. Non-Party Movements and Social Movements. The most popular time. At that time, many local forces. He even sneered at the Republic of China system. But in the subsequent 1990s. In the process of democratization. Instead, the Republic of China's system in Taiwan. Gained legitimacy.

When our president and legislators. All the people from Taiwan, Penghu, Kinmen and Matsu. Selected in a relatively transparent way. No matter how many flaws there are. At that time, it was also emphasized. The Republic of China is a foreign regime. It will become difficult to stand. After all, this is along the lines of democratization. The process of Taiwanization. At that time, there were many foreign experts and scholars. For our series. The world's first national system change process. Did a lot of research. For example, Japanese scholar Masatake Wakabayashi. This phenomenon is called. Taiwanization of the Republic of China. Some international law scholars also argue that. This democratization process. Taiwan through the Republic of China. Gained national independence. No need to declare independence. The future topics are name rectification and constitution making. Changing the Republic of China from within. To adapt the Republic of China to the needs of Taiwan. Even during the Chen Shui-bian administration. Some scholars also mentioned that the Republic of China in Taiwan. The Second Republic of the Republic of China. Of course there is also the term "Third Republic" But at that time. As Taiwan's local forces gradually grew stronger. Even the calls for Taiwan's independence are getting louder. The provincial groups. Fear of the extinction of the Republic of China. It can only be said that it is increasing. But to be frank. This is processing. Relations between the Republic of China and Taiwan. From the perspective of these so-called "Taiwanese" The Republic of China was. The Kuomintang retreated. Coming to Taiwan. They came to colonize Taiwan. In their eyes, it is an alien regime. But now it has become the Republic of China in Taiwan. Moreover, these local forces. I'm actually running for president. And we must try every possible way to choose. So naturally we have to deal with. Relations between the Republic of China and Taiwan. After all, everyone knows. Early 1970s. America was mired in the Vietnam War. Seeing the Soviet Union's growing power. To deal with the Soviet Union and resolve the Vietnam War. Adopted a policy of reconciliation with the Chinese Communist Party. 1971. Republic of China's seat in the United Nations. Replaced by the Chinese Communist Party. Its international status also declined rapidly. The Chinese Communist Party is internationally regarded as. The only legitimate government in China. The Republic of China in the International. Lost the basis for representing China. The Kuomintang's policy of unification of China. Instead, it became a restriction. The stumbling block to the development of our Republic of China. The CCP even uses the "One China" principle. Constantly belittling the international status of the Republic of China. Threatening our survival. The International Status of the Republic of China. In fact, it is just like cross-strait relations. It's our ruling authorities. Problems that must be faced directly. Of course after. When dealing with these issues. Because of the complicated relationship. with the Chinese Communist Party.

Let the relationship between the Republic of China and Taiwan. Hidden again. How to locate the Republic of China. Or the international status of "Taiwan"? This has always been a hot potato. After Chen Shui-bian came to power. Emphasis on the two-nation theory. As a result, cross-strait relations have reached a stalemate. Until the Ma Ying-jeou government came to power. One China advocacy for cross-strait relations. Treat Taiwan as a region, not a country. So at that time. The Kuomintang's "Republic of China Taiwan" The DPP’s “The Republic of China is Taiwan”. Even more so, "Republic of China Taiwan" These two sets of concepts confront each other. Let's discuss this first. Taiwan, Republic of China. The Kuomintang's claim is. According to the Constitution. Both sides of the Taiwan Strait belong to one China. Even in 1997. The constitution has been amended. Divided the Republic of China into two parts. Taiwan area is Taiwan, Penghu, Kinmen and Matsu. The mainland is. Territory other than Taiwan, Penghu, Kinmen and Matsu. Whether in Taiwan or mainland China. Under the Constitution, they all belong to one China. The Constitution of the Republic of China. It's actually written like this. Even though the Republic of China. and the People's Republic of China. Currently not affiliated. It is an objective fact. The current situation in the Taiwan Strait. It is also internationally recognized. But Brother Niu based on the Constitution. I still have to say one thing. "Communism and independence" are doomed to fail! Unification is unstoppable! In this case. Then why not amend the Constitution to suit the current situation? So the Kuomintang. I also cling to the Constitution. Since you feel that it does not conform to the current situation. Then let’s amend the constitution! But everyone knows. In today's international situation. And under the situation in the Taiwan Strait. Constitutional amendments related to cross-strait relations. It will only be the last straw that triggers a war. Any party that proposes constitutional amendment at this stage. It would just be simply irresponsible. So in 1992. Lee Teng-hui's Administration. Republic of China and People's Republic of China. Straits Exchange Foundation. Association for Relations Across the Taiwan Straits. That is what we call the Straits Exchange Foundation. and the Association for Relations Across the Taiwan Straits. Two sessions hold talks in Hong Kong. The result of this meeting was later called the 1992 Consensus. But everyone! Did you know that this meeting actually produced no results? This meeting was supposed to discuss. Some cooperation matters in the correspondence. between the two parties. During the negotiation process, Beijing demanded. Taiwan must accept. The One China Principle: Both Sides. of the Taiwan Straits belong to One China. The Chinese authorities believe. One China is the People's Republic of China. After the unification in the future. Taiwan will become a "special administrative region" under its jurisdiction.

We believe that "One China" Should refer to the Republic of China since its establishment in 1912. Its sovereignty extends to the whole of China. But the current governance. It only applies to Taiwan, Penghu, Kinmen and Matsu. Taiwan is part of China. But the mainland is also part of China. We are stating. One China principle signed. Write down the Republic of China calendar. This was unacceptable to the Beijing authorities. So back and forth many times. There is no result after all. As for how it appeared later. What about the term "1992 Consensus"? The term "1992 Consensus" originated from the 1999. Tang Shubei, then vice president of the China Association. for Relations Across the Taiwan Straits, claimed. The two sides of the Taiwan Strait had already discussed in 1992. Reaching a "One China Consensus" This is the first time that the above meeting content. The beginning of what is generally called “consensus”. Later, he served as Chairman of the Mainland Affairs Council. of the Executive Yuan. Su Qi on April 28, 2000. For the first time, the above meeting content. Generally referred to as the "1992 Consensus" And add the interpretation of "One China, Different Interpretations" But from the above statement. We can easily see. For the Chinese Communist Party. 1992. Main Aspects of Negotiation Results. It's about "One China" But the government of the Republic of China at that time. The emphasis is on "each expressing himself" But the CCP did not agree. The Chinese Communist Party authorities. summarized the 1992 Consensus as. Both sides of the Taiwan Strait adhere to the one-China principle. Working together for national reunification. Both sides jointly oppose. Two Chinas, One China, One Taiwan. Believe that there is only one China in the world. Government of the People's Republic of China. It is the only legitimate government representing all of China. Government of the People's Republic of China. Represents all of China. The only legitimate government. Taiwan is Chinese territory. An integral part. Therefore, in the CCP’s view. The 1992 Consensus cannot be interpreted as. One China, Different Interpretations. Nor can it be interpreted as. Can recognize the sovereignty and existence of the Republic of China. It is even more impossible to accept. One China is the Republic of China. The Republic of China is also China. Taiwan belongs to the Republic of China. Taiwan is a sovereign and independent country. Taiwan does not belong to the People's Republic of China, etc. These political statements and actions. Oppose Two Chinas, One China, One Taiwan. Chinese independence, Taiwanese independence, etc. The 1992 Consensus only has "One China" There is no "each table" However, under this premise. Former KMT Chairman Lien Chan. Also in 2005. Visited mainland China. With the General Secretary of the CPC Central Committee. After the meeting with Hu Jintao. The "1992 Consensus" was once again proposed. As for what they said. Is it the same 1992 consensus? No one knows.

Do you want to listen to what you are saying? I know that. Everyone is still confused. Brother Niu, let me give you an example. Talk about the past period. The Dragon Boat Festival rice dumplings are ready. The Kuomintang advocates. My zongzi is northern zongzi. The Chinese Communist Party advocates. His zongzi is southern zongzi. But the Kuomintang feels. Southern Zongzi is basically steamed rice cake. I don't want to admit that he is a zongzi. The Chinese Communist Party also advocates. Northern rice dumplings are basically 3D oily rice! But in order to have a good Dragon Boat Festival. We can still agree reluctantly. The other party is a zongzi! most of the time. It's an art not to reveal it. The 1992 Consensus has always been regarded as. The political foundation for the development of cross-strait relations. Under the unanimous recognition. of their respective "1992 Consensus" The two sides of the Taiwan Strait have achieved a closer economy. As well as cultural exchange and cooperation. Year 2008. Direct sea and air transport and direct mail service between the two sides. of the Taiwan Strait. Realizing the “Three Links”. year 2010. The two sides signed ECFA. Although I have stopped a lot of lists recently. They are holding the banner of the 1992 Consensus. In Singapore in 2015. The first Ma-Xi meeting was held. But the connotation of the "1992 Consensus" It seems to be changing. Early 2019. Xi Jinping, the leader of the Chinese Communist Party. On the 40th Anniversary of the "Message to Compatriots in Taiwan" Delivered a speech at the memorial meeting. He advocated cross-strait policy. Must be based on the "1992 Consensus" Reiterate the need to abide by the "one country, two systems" principle. Once Xi Jinping puts the 1992 Consensus. Interpretation of "One Country, Two Systems" The one country is of course the CCP. There is no room for individual expression. This also awakened the people. How come this seems to be the past? The 1992 Consensus advocated by the Kuomintang. is not quite the same. Discover the greatest consensus of the 1992 Consensus. There is no consensus. When it comes to "one country, two systems" The best example is Hong Kong. But Hong Kong has been. After the Basic Law was amended. It has also become a one country, one system. From Taiwan's public opinion to geopolitics. And recently, American arms dealers came to Taiwan. Looking for partners. There are also AI technology industries preparing to enter Taiwan. In these aspects. At present, the "1992 Consensus" Relatively large space. Maybe it's getting less and less. After talking about the Kuomintang’s point of view. Next, let’s talk about the Democratic Progressive Party! Democratic Progressive Party vs. Kuomintang. The "1992 Consensus" Another argument. Taiwan's future is determined by the Taiwanese people. The main point of this discussion is. Taiwan, Republic of China. A sovereign and independent country.

Not affiliated with the People's Republic of China. Sovereignty belongs to the 23 million people of Taiwan. Only the Taiwanese people. Have the right to decide Taiwan's future. The Republic of China is Taiwan. The Democratic Progressive Party believes. Taiwan can only throw off the shackles of China. To move towards a normal country. Whether you agree or not. Or the means to achieve this end. There are concerns that it may lead to war. But he said plain words. Your future is determined by you. they think. As the Chinese Communist Party authorities press forward. Forced the party to start. The Republic of China can't care. Also consider the voice of the Republic of Taiwan. Since it is not feasible to mention the Republic of China. It is even more impossible to mention the Republic of Taiwan. Taiwan's statement. It became a political ideal. The result of compromise with political reality. Let the Republic of China and Taiwan. The concept has become the same group. The new generation of Taiwanese young people identify with Taiwan. But it does not exclude the Republic of China. Related symbols. These symbols are gradually lost. History and territory with mainland China. Sovereign Link. The trend of "de-Sinicization" The concept of the Republic of China (Taiwan). The Republic of China is equal to Taiwan. Completely decoupled from One China and Chinese representation. Meaning Taiwan is not part of China. The concept of the Republic of China (Taiwan). It has gone beyond the framework of "One China" What the CCP thinks. Taiwan is part of China. The CCP believes that Taiwan. A province of the People's Republic of China. The Kuomintang believes. Taiwan is part of China. It is also part of the Republic of China. But the Democratic Progressive Party believes. that "Republic of China Taiwan" A sovereign and independent country. This concept combines the Republic of China and Taiwan. The three meanings of being a sovereign and independent country. In the Democratic Progressive Party’s view. Republic of China and Taiwan Independence. No longer mutually exclusive concepts. But we can tolerate each other. They cleverly turned the independence. of the Republic of China into. And Taiwan's independence. Concepts such as "One Country, Two Systems" and "1992 Consensus" Convert and label. Trying to make the Republic of China. Equivalent to Taiwan independence. One Country, Two Systems is equivalent to the 1992 Consensus. But legally speaking. The Republic of China does not mean that Taiwan is independent. One Country, Two Systems and the 1992 Consensus. It involves cross-strait relations and institutional arrangements. The two have very different meanings. The Democratic Progressive Party believes. They have pragmatically put the Republic of China, Taiwan. Becoming the mainstream discourse within the party. and in Taiwanese society. But the CCP still criticizes this as gradual Taiwan independence. It is believed that this will increase hostility between the two sides. of the Taiwan Strait. Firmly oppose any form of Taiwan independence. The Democratic Progressive Party may think.

Since it cannot be used internationally. The name of the Republic of China. The Chinese Communist Party also does not recognize the existence. of the Republic of China. For the safety of Taiwan. Avoid clear and present danger. The DPP was also unable to complete the referendum. The goal of establishing the Republic of Taiwan. Therefore, the Republic of China, Taiwan. Instead, it has become the biggest consensus in Taiwanese society. After discussing both sides. Finally, let's talk about. The International Status of the Republic of China. Taiwan transformed into the Republic of China. Following the Republic of China in mainland China. How different. and the discontinuity between them. this is very important. After all, the Chinese Communist Party claims that the Republic of China. It was destroyed as early as 1949. And he was eager to help the Republic of China compile history books. Everyone should know. Every time China changes dynasties. First things first. That is to compile history books. Then the Republic of China Taiwan. Disconnection from the Republic of China. In fact, it does not violate the CCP’s theory. On the one hand, the Chinese Communist Party claims. The Republic of China has been destroyed. But they also demanded that Taiwan abide by the Constitution. of the Republic of China. No name change, no Taiwan independence. This self-contradictory logic. They want to use the Republic of China to hold Taiwan. So in 2022. Speaker of the United States House of Representatives. Pelosi came to Taiwan and made a call. After the passage of the Taiwan Policy Act. Why is the CCP so angry? Jump up and launch the military exercise around Taiwan. Because of the U.S. Taiwan Policy Act. It will bring us closer to legal independence. Because now we are de facto independent. Neither party has advocated "legal independence" After the United States passed this law. There might actually be some changes. And it also makes the relationship between Taiwan and the United States. Transformed into a relationship between countries. This is what the CCP doesn't want to see. Clarifying the relationship between the Republic of China and Taiwan. It is also for our international space. Due to the one-China policy in the international. The Republic of China government is not recognized. We can represent China. If we want to use the Republic of China (Taiwan). Traveling internationally. It is necessary to point out the difference with the Republic of China. And I no longer want to represent China. It only represents the position of the Republic of China or Taiwan. Republic of China system. If you want to be Taiwanese. Constitutional reform is an inevitable path. Taiwan will need a. A constitution more in line with current conditions. In particular, the Taiwanization of the Republic of China system. The path of constitutional amendment and constitution making. It's almost unavoidable. I wonder what you think? alright! That's it for this episode. If you like. Please help us press the subscribe button and share it. If you have capable friends. You can also join our membership. Or click Super Thanks! In addition, our DC group is now open!

Welcome everyone to come in and chat. My study notes. See you next time. ByeBye！

====

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mmiypn7urww

討論到現在中華民國的處境

在名詞上可能會看到

中華民國在臺灣、中華民國是臺灣

又或是之前像是前總統蔡英文

現任總統賴清德提的「中華民國臺灣」

但他有什麼差別呢？

又為什麼會有這樣的差別？

而有這個「在」字的差別

到底大不大呢？

我知道有人可能會說

這都是政客他們的文字遊戲啦

討論的意義並不大

但其實實際上

並沒有想像的那麼簡單哦

今天就讓我們透過這個議題

來講講我們中華民國的現況吧！

#臺灣 #台灣 #中華民國 #中國 #中華民國臺灣 #Taiwan #公民 #社會 #歷史 #政治 #賴清德 #🇹🇼 #民進黨 #總統 #憲法 #憲政 #台獨 #臺獨 #台獨黨綱 #修憲 #中共 #互不隸屬 #國民黨 #民眾黨 #九二共識 #蔡英文 #馬英九 #李登輝

Polya Yang、柯誠譽、Rosemary 、阿里不打、Sion Ein、Roots Ko、Wu Pin-Ying、赤炎、懶虎提姆閣、Roy Li、KaHu Lau、葉小雲、李文傑、陳建宏、Louis Tseng、薩麥爾_MLBB、祈翠、Mariposa Bay、語禪、Krib Stek、張輔庭、whiskyhot886 、Chen Chong Lee、Charlie曾、蔡岳峰、Chung-Ming Wang、上善若水、林雋凱

📍特別感謝每位默默支持我們的朋友們，你們給的不只是錢財，更是一段友誼 當你像小蝦米一樣對抗大鯨魚，當你的敵人遠比你想像中強大的時候 發現了這個世界，其實有一群未曾相識卻力挺你的朋友，我想，這就是世界上最美好的感覺了
